deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—i.e., never articulates a rationale such as 'I want you to work through this yourself so you retain the reasoning skill.' The tutoring constraints are enforced but the pedagogical rationale is never stated.

In Transcript 3, the student successfully self-corrects by deciding to go back to the lecture notes rather than guessing (Turn 5-6), which is a methodological improvement. The tutor affirms this but never prompts reflection on what changed in the student's approach or why that was the right move.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts, the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic practice: it consistently elicits student reasoning before offering any framing, never gives submission-ready answers, builds on student progress, and maintains a supportive tone. The primary weaknesses are the absence of any explicit rationale for the tutoring approach (1.3.A.a) and a missed opportunity to prompt process reflection after the student's methodological improvement in Transcript 3 (1.3.C.a). No deductions were warranted for 1.1 or 1.2, as the tutor consistently guided rather than told, scaffolded appropriately, and acknowledged student contributions.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: No deductions. The tutor never provides near-submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently asks the student to generate key ideas before offering any framing. 1.2: No deductions. The tutor builds on each student's correct steps and uses diagnostic questions before redirecting. 1.3: Two deductions applied. First, across all three conversations the tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than answering directly—the rationale for the Socratic approach is never articulated (1.3.A.a, -2). Second, in Transcript 3 the student makes a clear methodological improvement (deciding to check the lecture notes rather than guess), but the tutor simply affirms and moves on without prompting reflection on what changed or why that was the right approach (1.3.C.a, -2). Sections 2 and 3 are clean: the tutor stays on-task, redirects appropriately, keeps responses concise and interactive, and maintains a consistently supportive, non-evaluative tone.
